paper_title: Using Astragalus to Model Body Mass, Habitat Preference, and Population Density of Prehistoric Bovid Duboisia santeng (Dubois, 1891) in Eastern Java island in The Early Pleistocene

methods.description: Procedure for locating, collecting, and measuring specific morphometric parameters (width and lateral length) of fossilized astragalus bones.

steps.description: The fossils in the form of astragalus bones were found in Trinil area located near the river bank ( Figure 1) in the north of Ngawi.
steps.searchable_summary: Locate and collect astragalus bone fossils from the Trinil area.

steps.description: The bones then were measured following Degusta and Drba (2013). The parameters taken from the bones measurements were width (W) and lateral length (LI) in mm ( Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure width (W) and lateral length (Ll) of collected astragalus bones.
outputs: Measured parameters (Width, Lateral Length) for each bone (in mm)

methods.description: Estimating the body mass of D. santeng using measured astragalus lateral length and an allometric equation derived from Tsubamoto (2014). Includes a comparative step with extant species.

steps.description: First, this step is by comparing the D. santeng width and lateral length with other extant Bovid species. By using this comparison, D. santeng can be compared whether the body mass related to which living Bovids.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare D. santeng astragalus measurements (W, Ll) with measurements from extant Bovid species.
inputs: Database/measurements of extant Bovid astragalus (W, Ll)
outputs: Insights into relatedness to extant Bovids

steps.description: Then, the body mass was estimated by using allometric equation developed by Tsubamoto (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply allometric equation to estimate body mass from astragalus measurements.

steps.description: The astragalus lateral length (Ll) -body mass linear model was using slope value of 2.789 and intercept of 2.078. In this linear model, the lateral length was used as the predictor and the body mass was the result.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate body mass using the linear model: Body Mass = 2.789 * Ll + 2.078.

methods.method_name: Population Density Estimation from Body Mass
methods.description: Estimating the population density of D. santeng using the estimated body mass and a linear model developed by Damuth (1981).

steps.description: The linear model was also used to estimate the population density of D. santeng. The model was followed model developed by Damuth (1981).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply linear model to estimate population density from estimated body mass.

steps.description: The body mass -population density linear model was using slope value of -0.75 and intercept of 4.23. In this linear model, the body mass was used as the predictor (Tsubamoto 2014) and the population density (individuals/km 2 ) was the result.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate population density using the linear model: Population Density = -0.75 * Body Mass + 4.23.

methods.description: Modeling the spatial distribution of the estimated population density across the Ngawi landscape as a function of forest cover.

steps.description: The spatial model of the population density in Ngawi landscape was the function of forest covers. The whole process of population density using the astragalus bones can be seen in Figure 4.
steps.searchable_summary: Model the spatial distribution of estimated population density based on forest cover data.
inputs: Forest cover data for Ngawi landscape
outputs: Spatial model of population density distribution
